reasoning: Both models correctly identified the PKGBUILD contains only static variable assignments, verified source URLs, and safe install commands. No build/install hooks or risky operations exist.
coverage_issues: Both models failed to review the downloaded binary (winload) itself, though this is beyond standard AUR audit scope
No .install scripts exist in package, so gap is minimal
false_negatives: Models didn't verify if binary matches checksums in practice (theoretical risk)
No analysis of the actual binary's behavior during execution
learnings: Improve file selection: Tools must prioritize reviewing .install hooks if present; current logic missed this (though none existed here)
Assessment quality: Models correctly focused on build/install phases but over-explained irrelevant details (e.g., qwen's GitHub trust discussion)
Failure mode: Binary packages need clearer handling – tools should explicitly note they don't audit precompiled binaries
Coverage gap: Audit reports should explicitly state if .install scripts are absent to prove full coverage
